ogTitle: Carcuma vs Carey Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Carcuma and Carey Gully.
heading: Carcuma vs Carey Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Carcuma, South Australia and Carey Gully, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Carey Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Carcuma (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Carcuma or Carey Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Carcuma has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Carey Gully scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Carcuma scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Carey Gully scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Carcuma or Carey Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Carey Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
